title: Tinker's Knives
description: Reskins Tinkers' Construct Daggers in the style of Farmer's Delight Knives, with compatibility for Tinkers' Integrations and Tweaks
body:
# Requirements
### [Tinkers' Construct](https://modrinth.com/mod/tinkers-construct) (Required)
Since this Resourcepack reskins Tinkers' Construct Daggers, Tinkers' Construct is obviously required, otherwise this resourcepack doesn't do anything.
### [Farmer's Delight](https://modrinth.com/mod/farmers-delight) (Optional)
This Resourcepack was made with Farmer's delight in mind, though Farmer's Delight and Tinkers' Construct do not have base compatibility with each other, you will need a datapack to add Tinkers' Construct Daggers under the "forge:tools/knives" tag, or get this next mod
### [Tinkers Delight](https://modrinth.com/mod/tinkers-construct-delight) (Optional)
Tinkers Delight adds compatibility between Farmer's Delight and Tinkers' Construct, as well as adding new recipes and foods.

If you like compatibility with Farmer's Delight, you should check out their other stuff, sadly most of their Farmer's Delight compats aren't on Modrinth, so you'll have to look up their Curseforge profile.

Incase you do not want to get Tinkers Delight, I'd reccomend adding compatibility with Farmer's Delight through a datapack as I explained above, that is if you're going to be using Farmer's Delight with Tinkers.
### [Tinkers' Integrations and Tweaks](https://modrinth.com/mod/tcintegrations) (Optional)
A great Tinkers' Construct addon with an eye for balanced integration, this resourcepack also adds reskins for their additions such as Neptunium (from Aquaculture) and Brass (from Create).

Check out their other mods too, their same eye for balance is present in all their other mods, making their mods worth considering for any well-balanced modpack.

# Description
After testing NocubeYT's Tinkers Delight addon in a modpack, I noticed it would be pretty easy to reskin the Tinkers' Construct daggers into knives as they only utilized 2 parts.

At first, This was just supposed to be for personal use in a modpack I'm making for friends, but I decided to contribute to modrinth since I support the idea of an open-source modding platform.

Moreover, I feel many see Tinkers' Construct as a fossil of a mod and I hope to help make it feel just a little more up-to-date by throwing my hat into the ring and making some visual integration between 2 of my most favorite mods.

# Versions
Though this resourcepack was made for 1.19.2, it should work for all versions except those before 1.16.5, and it should even work on any new versions of tinkers unless they completely rework the tools again (which I assume they won't) if it shows a warning about the resourcepack being made for a newer or older version, just ignore it.

If you have any issues or suggestions simply contact me in DMs on my discord (since modrinth doesn't do comments) my username and icon are the same ones that I have here and my DMs are open to the public
